essence: David reviews overnight dark-factory prototypes: visually good Mochaccino-styled static HTML pages with realistic data, but none built a Claude Agent SDK / KyberAgent extension and the inline agents only exercised the already-working Claude Code harness, not the dark factory harness itself.

actions.text: Build prototypes as KyberAgent / Claude Agent SDK extensions that exercise the dark factory harness rather than only the Claude Code harness

essence: Cleaned duplicate of the same capture: prototypes are static HTML pages (not server-backed apps), Mochaccino styling praised, but no KyberAgent/Claude Agent SDK extension was built and the work demonstrates the Claude Code harness rather than testing the dark factory harness.

David decides KDD working/archive files should live in a .kdd-style dotfolder inside each project (not the Cortex-style location previously reused) until the dedicated KDD home exists within hours, stresses Lisa/KDD matters more than Lexi, asks for a pasteable high-level synopsis, and floats an OMI-fetch extension plus a KDD at-a-glance inspection extension (projects, decisions, documents, patterns).

actions.text: Adopt a .kdd dotfolder inside the project as interim home for KDD working/archive files, then migrate when the KDD home exists
Write a pasteable high-level synopsis of the KDD structure for sharing
Research a KDD inspection extension showing projects, decisions, documents and patterns at a glance

essence: David frames the micro-app push: apps connected via APIs/MCPs give the dark factory terminal agentic access to sessions and tickets, WatchTower and Switchboard are the David-to-agent communication layer, and imperfect builds are acceptable because the learning and documentation must be banked before Fable access ends in about six days.

actions.text: Propose the prioritized list of micro apps/extensions to build, with reasons and how they connect
Document the WatchTower and Switchboard communication flow

essence: With Fable 5 re-released on both Claude plans, David sets the 7-day dark factory goal: a communication-first autonomous system (voice in/out, visible tickets, alert chimes, morning summaries across 10-15 projects), separating the main dark-factory folder from PoC and third-party reference code, architecture docs visualized via Mochaccino-style schema+data plus image generation, balanced single-responsibility micro-apps, and a Lisa upgrade that extracts ideas (not just lessons/patterns) with provenance chains into reviewable PRs.

actions.text: Create dark factory architecture documentation covering events, hooks, pipelines and communication channels
Define a visualization system for dark factory apps, tickets and morning status summaries

essence: David recalls a years-old Ruby TUI prompt-runner (possibly in appydave-tools) with cron-like pacing that batch-executes image prompts unattended, and proposes reviving it as a KyberAgent extension that injects prompts into the focused window to execute thumbnail prompt batches for free, noting the thumbnail axes matrix (~100 combos) must be shortlisted to 10-20 since YouTube only uses about 3.

actions.text: Locate the old TUI image-prompt processing app in the Ruby gems / appydave-tools codebase

essence: David scopes a thumbnail-generator micro app built as a KyberAgent extension combining the recipe skill with the thumbnail-design documentation, starting with the AppyDave brand then AI-TLDR, vOz and Kybernesis, with per-brand rule documents (subliminal/ghost text, thumbnail text, title focus, transcript-derived fields), later preloading data from recording/title workflows, and delivering a regenerable extension recipe.

actions.text: Scope the thumbnail generator micro app as a KyberAgent extension
Define thumbnail generator fields and per-brand rules starting with AppyDave
Review the Kyber Extension SDK before implementation

essence: David commissions a generalized, self-improving recipe skill — recipes as IKEA-flat-pack-style instruction sets (with or without parts) where a harness (AppyStack, Kybernesis extensions) supplies infrastructure so the recipe carries only the domain problem — able to create, read, structure, modify and customize recipes with guided Q&A for clients, grounded in research of his past recipe conversations plus examples like OpenAI's Symphony and the LLM-brain recipe.

actions.text: Research David's past recipe-development conversations and external examples, then build the generalized self-improving recipe skill

essence: David thinks through the Kyber Extension SDK ecosystem — pub/sub host-extension communication, an app/port registry currently conflated with locations.json, turning existing apps (AngelEye, AppyRadar, FliVideo, Storyline) into extensions, optional third-party hosts (AionUI, Theo's T3) as decoupling insurance — and lands on a monetization strategy of selling recipes (core app logic plus visualization surface) via community or paywall, plus an Airtable-style relational-data extension idea.

actions.text: Install and test latest AionUI and T3 as candidate third-party extension hosts
Build an app/port registry extension (apps, locations, ports) separate from locations.json

essence: Over lunch David and a Kybernesis peer discuss bringing Angela (disability domain expert whose three-way app partnership imploded, now AI-pilled) into the consulting pipeline as an external consultant — she is researching an Anthropic-leader path for Australia — while the peer starts standardizing pricing and pipeline on zero revenue, and they compare second-brain designs: Camp A (DB-as-truth) vs Camp B (file-as-truth), the peer's OMI ingestion/retrieval routers with temporal and entity extraction, and David's session-archaeology skill.

actions.text: Decide who leads the Angela consulting engagement and where the Kybernesis consulting line sits (peer to discuss with Ian)

essence: Chiang Mai AI meetup debates GPT-5.6's architecture-embedded layered safeguard stack and US government gating of model releases (Fable pulled days after launch, Meta the last regulatory holdout), concluding businesses need fallback models, proprietary evals and multi-provider robustness, illustrated by Shopify's aggressive AI-native engineering pivot (rate-of-learning focus, pair programming, FDE-style roles).

actions.text: Review the Shopify head-of-engineering AI strategy video from the Cursor event

essence: Chiang Mai AI meetup dissects the GPT-5.6 family (Soul/Tara/Luna) — Soul claiming Mythos-level performance at a third of the tokens and far lower price but available only to ~20 trusted partners, ultra mode as harness-embedded sub-agent orchestration — plus practical engineering notes on dependency/container security scanning, prompt-caching costs, and Claude Code session branch-vs-fork workflows.
